title: National Historical Museum
subTitle: Muzeu Historik Kombëtar
description: Major museum in a Communist-era building featuring themed exhibitions about the history of Albania.

peopleAlsoSearch.title: Museum of Natural Sciences Sabiha Kasimati

updatesFromCustomers.text: Beeindruckendes Mosaik auf der Fassade des Historischen Nationalmusem.

reviews.name: Samed A
reviews.text: İskenderbey Meydanı’nı gezerken gördüğümüz fakat tadilat dolayısıyla kapalı olan Arnavutluk Ulusal Tarihi’nin işlendiği bir müzeymiş. Kapalı olduğu için ziyaret edemedik ama dışarıdan güzel duruyordu. Fotoğraftaki aynı resmi Üsküp şehir merkezinde de gördük.
reviews.textTranslated: While strolling through Skanderbeg Square, we saw a museum dedicated to the national history of Albania, but it was closed for renovations. We couldn't visit it because it was closed, but it looked beautiful from the outside. We saw the same painting in the city center of Skopje as well.

reviews.name: Gert Peshkatari
reviews.text: Its closed for a major renovation bit its a landmark for Tirana. Must visit when it reopens.

reviews.name: Suzana Ravs (Nana)
reviews.text:
National Historical Museum je odličen kraj za spoznavanje bogate zgodovine Albanije. Muzej skozi številne razstave in zgodovinske predmete predstavi razvoj države od antičnih časov do sodobnosti. Posebej izstopa velika mozaik fasada, ki je ena najbolj prepoznavnih podob Tirane.
Razstave so zanimive in raznolike, zato je obisk primeren tako za ljubitelje zgodovine kot za vse, ki želijo bolje razumeti albansko kulturo in preteklost. Lokacija v središču mesta omogoča enostaven obisk med raziskovanjem Tirane. Priporočam obisk vsem, ki jih zanimata zgodovina in kultura Balkana.
reviews.textTranslated: The National Historical Museum is a great place to learn about the rich history of Albania. Through numerous exhibitions and historical artifacts, the museum presents the development of the country from ancient times to modern times. The large mosaic facade, which is one of the most recognizable images of Tirana, stands out. The exhibitions are interesting and diverse, so a visit is suitable for both history lovers and anyone who wants to better understand Albanian culture and the past. The location in the city center makes it easy to visit while exploring Tirana. I recommend a visit to anyone interested in the history and culture of the Balkans.

reviews.name: Federico Cazzadore
reviews.text: Bella facciata
reviews.textTranslated: Beautiful facade

reviews.name: laurence
reviews.text: Fermé depuis des lustres pour rénovation
reviews.textTranslated: Closed for ages for renovation

reviews.name: Dr Maaruf Ali
reviews.text: A comprehensive museum of everything Albanian covering prehistory to the modern world. Many exhibts are preserved to see. You can easily spend almost a whole day there. It is not free to enter.

reviews.name: yasmin sema
reviews.text: Kapalı olduğu için müzeyi ziyaret edemedim.
reviews.textTranslated: I couldn't visit the museum because it was closed.

title: House of Leaves Museum
openingHoursBusinessConfirmationText: Confirmed by phone call 2 weeks ago

updatesFromCustomers.text: An amazing experience discover albanian history through this wonderful museum . It feels like stuck in time while you are visiting it . So many beautiful things you can find there and learn more about a dark time of albanian history that still yesterday was unfound.

reviews.name: Robert Ceka
reviews.text: Duhet ta vizitojnë patjetër sta që duan të mësojnê më shumë për funksionimin e policisë sekrete dhe survejimit të njerëzve në kohën e regjimit komunist në Shqipëri. Ambientet janë kuruar me kujdes dhe ekspozitorë të pasur. Çmimi i biletës paksa i shtrenjtë krahasuar me muzetë e tjerë në rajon.
reviews.textTranslated: A must-visit for those who want to learn more about the functioning of the secret police and the surveillance of people during the communist regime in Albania. The premises are carefully curated and the exhibits are rich. The ticket price is a bit expensive compared to other museums in the region.

reviews.name: Dominik Ciebiera
reviews.text:
Budynek historyczny i z ciekawą historią zarówno tą komunistyczną jak i przed.

Niestety muzeum bardzo słabe. Troszkę bez pomysłu i niewiele się z niego wynosi.

Pomijając sam budynek znacznie lepiej niemal to samo jest opowiedziane w Bunk'Art kilkadziesiąt metrów dalej.
reviews.textTranslated:
A historic building with an interesting history, both communist and pre-communist.

Unfortunately, the museum is very weak. It's a bit uninspired, and there's not much to be learned from it.

Aside from the building itself, almost the same story is told much better at Bunk'Art, a few dozen meters away.

reviews.name: Piergiuseppe Laera (PL)
reviews.text: Da vedere se siete affascinati dalla storia contemporanea.
reviews.textTranslated: A must-see if you are fascinated by contemporary history.

reviews.name: Sharlene Golding
reviews.text:
Don't forget to do the onsite outdoor bunker!!
A great overview of Albanias saddest years.

reviews.name: liala salvetti
reviews.text: Era chiuso.
reviews.textTranslated: It was closed.

reviews.name: Riccardo Letizia
reviews.text: Museo storico che rappresenta la situazione del paese sotto la dittatura
reviews.textTranslated: Historical museum representing the situation of the country under the dictatorship

reviews.name: Federico Cazzadore
reviews.text: In centro
reviews.textTranslated: In the center

reviews.text: C’est une mosquée ottomane dans le bazar de Tirana. Pas pu le visiter. Elle ́n’ouvre apparemment qu'aux heures de prières. A côté une bâtisse pour les ablutions.
reviews.textTranslated: It's an Ottoman mosque in the Tirana bazaar. I couldn't visit it. Apparently, it only opens during prayer times. Next to it is a building for ablutions.

reviews.name: Gonzalo Gutierrez Vega
reviews.text: Ciudad encantadora para perderse en ella y disfrutar de sus vetustas callejuelas y admirar las edificaciones más modernas que se levantan por doquier. Atractivo turístico de primer orden. Hay que visitarlo
reviews.textTranslated: A charming city to lose yourself in, enjoy its ancient streets, and admire the most modern buildings that rise everywhere. A top tourist attraction. A must-see.

reviews.name: Liliana Laurentia Enache
reviews.text: Cita profuma di tiglio in fiore ...mi sono sentita come a casa...
reviews.textTranslated: Cita smells of linden blossom...I felt right at home...

reviews.text: Una hermosa escultura dedicada a la paz, muy cerca de la pirámide y rodeada de bellos jardines. Un buen punto para hacer tus fotografías de Tirana.
reviews.textTranslated: A beautiful sculpture dedicated to peace, very close to the pyramid and surrounded by lovely gardens. A great spot to take photos of Tirana.

reviews.text: Pamätník síce len na krátku zastávku, ale s hlbokým symbolickým vyznamom.
reviews.textTranslated: The monument is only a short stop, but it has a deep symbolic meaning.

description: Opened in 1988 as the Enver Hoxha Museum, this large pyramidal structure is now a conference center.
address: Bulevardi Dëshmorët e Kombit 5, Tiranë, Albania

updatesFromCustomers.text: Nice view from the Clock Tower of Tirana

reviews.text: Ładnie widać panorame centrum, polecam. Schody strome. Na wieży gołebie i ich odchody ale pani w kasie oferuje płyn do desynfekcji rąk.

reviews.text: Cant pay with card in 2026,what a shame

updatesFromCustomers.text: The old loco was apparently painted and spruced up a few years ago. It needs some more of that.

reviews.text: Cool train, there is also a tank across the road!

reviews.name: Deividas Pustelninkas
reviews.text: Senas lokomotyvas, kitoje kelio pusėje yra tankas :)

Uma locomotiva que deveria ser melhor cuidada.
Vale a pena visitar se estiver por perto.
reviews.textTranslated:
A locomotive that should be better cared for.
Worth a visit if you're nearby.

reviews.name: Romeo Metko
reviews.text: interesant si instalacion

reviews.name: Jim Shaw
reviews.text: It's always worth seeing these relics from a bygone era. Nowadays, the loco is just used by kids to climb on and off. The authorities in Tirana should have it repainted and spruced up. If it was worth saving from the breaker's yard previously, it's worth another lick of paint today.

reviews.name: Jim Snodgrass
reviews.text: It's a fairly old and worn locomotive

reviews.name: Dorsey Kindler
reviews.text: Nice locomotive!

title: Bunk'Art 2
subTitle: BUNK'ART 2
description: Unique Albanian history museum inside a preserved, Communist-era nuclear pit bunker.

updatesFromCustomers.text: Bunker 2 in Tirana City Centre,  didn't go inside this year but it is an interesting experience seeing it in such a location.

reviews.name: Adam
reviews.text:
This is by far the most important highlight of Albania. I not only strongly recommend it to any visitor.

I also appeal to the Albanian authorities to change the name of the object, because Bunk'Art 1 and 2 is misleading. Both are not at all about Art, rather the opposite of Art. Art needs freedom and this place is about how a horror regime systematically destroyed anything of worth of its own nation, including torturing and executing artists. "Museum of Albanian Communism" should be the proper name for the clandestine Communist Command Center in case of a hot war against any enemy.

reviews.name: Steffi E.
reviews.text: Sehr interessant, sollte bei einem Tirana-Besuch Pflicht sein.
reviews.textTranslated: Very interesting, should be a must-see when visiting Tirana.

reviews.name: TreborMalaya KindGiraffe
reviews.text: Kept me engaged with all of the different rooms and English explanations.  Also learned a TON about Albania's 20th-century history.

reviews.name: Gijs Schutmaat
reviews.text: interessant en zeer de moeite waard. Must see!!!
reviews.textTranslated: Interesting and well worth the effort. Must see!!!

reviews.name: Nik
reviews.text: Wir waren an einem regnerischen Tag dort. Daher war es UNFASSBAR voll. Man musste bei jedem kleinen Raum anstehen. Hinzu kommt, dass alle Wände mit Text vollgeklatscht sind. Das ist so unglaublich viel, dass das wirklich keiner Bock hat zu lesen. Es ist dazu noch super eng und stickig. Den letzten Raum fanden wir sehr interessant - da wurde darüber berichtet, wie Albaner Juden im 2. Weltkrieg vor den nazis beschützt haben. Sehr rührend. Allerdings verstehe ich nicht, wie man direkt im Anschluss an diesen emotionalen Raum einen Random Raum mit optischen Illusionen ranhängt. Etwas geschmacklos und total thematisch unpassend 😂
reviews.textTranslated: We were there on a rainy day, so it was incredibly crowded. You had to queue for every little room. On top of that, all the walls were plastered with text. There was so much that nobody really wanted to read it. It was also super cramped and stuffy. We found the last room very interesting – it was about how Albanians protected Jews from the Nazis during World War II. Very moving. However, I don't understand why they immediately followed such an emotional room with a random room full of optical illusions. A bit tasteless and totally out of place thematically. 😂

reviews.name: Doğan Siyah
reviews.text: güzel aydınlatma ancak görülecek pek birşeyi yok açıkçası.
reviews.textTranslated: The lighting is nice, but honestly, there's not much to see.

Not much.

title: Skanderbeg Square
subTitle: Sheshi Skënderbej
description: Prominent central square featuring a national hero monument & surrounding historic buildings.
address: Plaza Tirana, Sheshi Skender Beu, Tiranë 1001, Albania

updatesFromCustomers.text: La piazza principale della città da cui si può arrivare con pochi passi a tutte le principali attrazioni. Ospita al suo interno il Museo Nazionale della Storia (ad oggi chiuso), il Teatro dell'Opera, la statua di Skënderbeg, la scritta  I love Tirana, ha nelle prossime vicinanze la Moschea Et'hem Bej e la torre dell'orologio.

reviews.name: Diederick van Oorschot
reviews.text: Een plein
reviews.textTranslated: A square

reviews.name: Ebrar ERDOĞAN
reviews.text: Eski ve yeni şehrin karışımı.
reviews.textTranslated: A blend of old and new city.

reviews.name: Christy Miller
reviews.text:
Gigantic and peaceful. A fabulous tribute to a national hero.
Not to be missed.

reviews.name: beauty sandra igene
reviews.text: I'll visit again. There's a construction ongoing around the square. Theres an event towards May ending. I love T sign is gated. You can't take clear pictures at the moment. Around the square you have the museum, I love EU sign . The mountain . Intercontinental hotel is an iconic landmark. There's a long road by the square beautiful and alot of restaurants around. I highly recommend Tirana. I'll visit again.

reviews.name: Alessandro Castagna • 3 2 1
reviews.text: Si sente la storia e l’arte che contraddistingue questo paese, un luogo contornato da elementi contrastanti tra cultura e passato tragico
reviews.textTranslated: You can feel the history and art that distinguishes this country, a place surrounded by contrasting elements between culture and a tragic past.

title: Tirana Lake Park
subTitle: Parku i Liqenit të Tiranës
description: Spacious public park with a botanical garden, zoo, the Presidential Palace, a church & a lake.
imageCategories: Statues and Cenotaphs of the Frashëri Brothers (Abdyl, Naim, Sami)

reviews.name: G Walker
reviews.text: Must visit, beautiful and absolutely massive park filled with monuments, benches and even some shops. Morning and evening walks here are highly recommended.

reviews.name: shiaowei z
reviews.text: This is a great park, it’s quite big so it’s easy to spend a lot of time here. I haven’t been here many times but everytime I go, I thoroughly enjoy my experience. Although the lake looks kind of funky….i still really appreciate the existence of the lake. tiny warning, the public restrooms throughout the park are like the craziest looking toilets I have ever seen in my life so just please please pee before u come here. If you have ever seen Trainspotting, the toilets here are comparable to that one scene. Anyways, this is a WONDERFUL picnic spot in tirana

reviews.name: Caterina Masala
reviews.text: Un sito molto bello, pulito e suggestivo
reviews.textTranslated: A very beautiful, clean and suggestive site

reviews.name: Agi Máslo
reviews.text: Krasne misto na piknik nebo nejakou vychazku. Zachody jsem moc nepochopila, ale nejake tam jsou. Jsou tu i nejake kavarny a restaurace.
reviews.textTranslated: A beautiful place for a picnic or a walk. I didn't really understand the sunsets, but there are some. There are also some cafes and restaurants.

reviews.name: Radha Hirani
reviews.text: Lovely place for walk

title: Tanners' Bridge
subTitle: Ura e Tabakëve
description: Former 18th-century stone livestock bridge, now restored for use by pedestrians.

peopleAlsoSearch.title: Ura e këmbësorëve te lagjja e "Tabakëve"
Ura prej guri në lagjen e Tabakëve Shek. XVIII
Ura mbi Lanë te Gjykata e Tiranës

reviews.name: Jose GN
reviews.text: Muy curioso y transitable
reviews.textTranslated: Very interesting and walkable

reviews.name: fabio flinstons
reviews.text: Non è tanto valorizzato. Un semplice ponte in pietra (anche scivolosa se bagnato) a cunetta tra le strade di Tirana
reviews.textTranslated: It's not much appreciated. A simple stone bridge (slippery when wet) that runs along the streets of Tirana.

reviews.name: gabin oudot
reviews.text: Pont assez décevant malheureusement. Il n’est pas du tout mis en valeur et semble assez peu entretenu, ce qui est vraiment dommage pour un lieu avec un certain intérêt historique. Avec un meilleur aménagement et davantage d’entretien, l’endroit pourrait être beaucoup plus agréable à visiter.
reviews.textTranslated: Unfortunately, the bridge is rather disappointing. It's not showcased at all and seems quite neglected, which is a real shame for a place with some historical interest. With better landscaping and more upkeep, the area could be much more pleasant to visit.

reviews.name: János Kiss
reviews.text: A Tímárok hídja egy kis ékszerdoboz Tirana szívében, ahol a múlt és a jelen látványosan találkozik. Ez a 18. századi, oszmán stílusú kőhíd egykor a várost a keleti hegyvidékekkel összekötő Szent György-karavánút kulcsfontosságú pontja volt, és nevét a környéken megtelepedett bőrművesekről kapta. Különleges élmény, hogy a híd ma is szabadon látogatható, és valóban át lehet rajta sétálni. Ahogy az ember a régi, egyenetlen kövekre lép, egy pillanatra kiszakad a modern város nyüzsgéséből, és szinte érezni lehet a történelem súlyát a talpa alatt. Bár a Lana folyó már nem alatta folyik, a híd restaurálása és környezete segít elképzelni, milyen lehetett itt az élet évszázadokkal ezelőtt.
reviews.textTranslated: The Bridge of Tanners is a little jewel in the heart of Tirana, where the past and the present meet in a spectacular way. This 18th-century, Ottoman-style stone bridge was once a key point on the St. George Caravan Road, which connected the city to the eastern highlands, and was named after the tanners who settled in the area. It is a special experience that the bridge is still open to the public today and you can actually walk across it. As you step onto the old, uneven stones, you are momentarily removed from the hustle and bustle of the modern city and you can almost feel the weight of history beneath your feet. Although the Lana River no longer flows beneath it, the restoration of the bridge and its surroundings help you imagine what life was like here centuries ago.

reviews.name: Tomas Z
reviews.text: Tanners bridge is a small historic stone bridge you can see from the side of a road. It’s no longer used but restored nicely to retain the history. It was built to go over a river that no longer exists there as it was diverted decades ago. The area is nice and leafy with some local cafes nearby, but I wouldn’t go out of my way to see it.

reviews.name: Maja Valencia
reviews.text: Excelente destino para 2 o 3 dias...muy seguro
reviews.textTranslated: Excellent destination for 2 or 3 days...very safe

reviews.name: Laurence Chambert
reviews.text:
Ville mélangeant toutes les époques, toutes les architectures, très innovatrice.
Beaucoup trop de constructions en cours.
reviews.textTranslated:
A city blending all eras and architectural styles, very innovative.

Far too many construction projects underway.

reviews.name: Araceli Martinez
reviews.text: Tirana es una capital no muy grande que con un día se puede ver perfectamente lo más importante. Se está desarrollando y haciendo grandes construcciones
reviews.textTranslated: Tirana is a relatively small capital city; you can easily see the most important sights in a day. It is currently developing and undergoing major construction projects.

reviews.name: Giusy Bonaccorsi
reviews.text: Amo tirana..
reviews.textTranslated: I love Tirana..

reviews.name: Netsayi Mwoyounazvo
reviews.text: Tirana great place to visit. Nice people only language barrier sometimes as most people does not understand English but you will be able to find one or two who can help.  Area feels safe people out late night. Need to know areas to visit for entertainment and transportation system.  End up relaying on taxi as it was difficult to ask and find out about public transport due to language issues. But in overall I will come back, a nice place to visit

reviews.name: Françoise Cardinaux
reviews.text: Une ville magnifique qui renaît de ses cendres. Beaucoup de choses à découvrir. Une ville où il fait bon vivre.
reviews.textTranslated: A magnificent city rising from the ashes. So much to discover. A city where life is good.

reviews.name: Danica Zubić
reviews.text:
27.06.25.
Tirana očigledno zadnjih godina se razvija u moderan kako industrijski grad (iz pravca Drača je industrijska zona duža od 10 km) tako i turistički. U centru na Skenderbegov- om trgu vidi se spoj modernog i starog tradicionalnog. Slično je i u ostalim delovima grada. Moderni trgovački centri i bunkeri.
reviews.textTranslated:
27.06.25.
Tirana has obviously been developing in recent years into a modern industrial city (from the direction of Durres there is an industrial zone over 10 km long) and a tourist one. In the center on Skenderbeg Square, you can see a combination of modern and old traditional. It is similar in other parts of the city. Modern shopping centers and bunkers.

updatesFromCustomers.text: Best night ever please goooo it's so worth it

reviews.name: Renu Patel
reviews.text: Absolutely wonderful performance with a truly wonderful cast who put their heart and soul into it. Loved every moment. Don’t miss it!

reviews.name: Ummulkulthum Kabiru Turaki
reviews.text: lovely performance

reviews.name: Noelle Briand
reviews.text: Great show

reviews.name: Richard Andhika
reviews.text: What a way to end our last avond at Tirana, immersive and entertaining. Time flies by. You all did a good job!!

reviews.name: buffi hendrix
reviews.text: Best part of our visit to Albania! Do not miss this show.

reviews.name: Mackenzie Carr
reviews.text: SO FUN. A must do for people visiting Albania!

reviews.name: Janki Patel
reviews.text: Amazing experience we had over here

reviews.name: Gage Mele
reviews.text: Absolutely amazing experience! Beautiful show of song and dance! Can’t recommend enough, an absolute must see if visiting Albania

reviews.name: Taz Anam
reviews.text: Amazing experience

reviews.name: Edna AKAKPO
reviews.text: Amazing!!!

title: Love Lock
imageCategories: Statues and Cenotaphs of the Frashëri Brothers (Abdyl, Naim, Sami)

reviews.name: Nicholas
reviews.text: The pic show it all..  sailboat with hundreds of locks.  The lake and park around it is nice to walk around, but it's big and takes over an hour.

reviews.name: Simone Pisciotta
reviews.text: Bellissimo lago dentro tirana
reviews.textTranslated: Beautiful lake inside Tirana
